data.name: DescHome
data.value:
SYS_OPTIMIZER is a maintenance tool designed to free up disk space and keep your system clean by removing unnecessary files that Windows accumulates over time.
AVAILABLE MODULES: You will find 6 cleaning modules organized by access level. Basic modules (Temp Files, Basic Disk) do not require special permissions. Advanced modules (Deep Temp, Prefetch, Event Log, Adv Disk) require running the app as Administrator.
BEFORE YOU START — RECOMMENDATIONS: > Create a system restore point before running any module. Control Panel → System → System Protection → Create. > Close all open programs to avoid conflicts with files in use. > Do not interrupt the process once it has started.
DISCLAIMER: The use of this tool is entirely the user's responsibility. The developer is not responsible for data loss, system issues or any damage resulting from the use of this application. Proceed at your own risk.

data.name: DescTemp
data.value:
Deletes temporary files stored in %TEMP%. Windows and your apps create these files while they run — things like leftover install files, session data and cached information that is no longer needed.
RISK: LOW These files are meant to be temporary. Windows and your apps will recreate them automatically when needed. Some files may be in use by running programs and will be skipped automatically.
TIP: Close all programs before running this module to remove as many files as possible.

data.name: DescDisk
data.value:
Runs a basic disk cleanup by removing three types of leftover files: your Recycle Bin, Windows thumbnail cache and Windows error reports generated by apps that have crashed in the past.
RISK: LOW Emptying the Recycle Bin is permanent — make sure you do not need to recover anything before running. Thumbnails are rebuilt automatically the next time you browse a folder with images. Error reports are diagnostic logs that do not affect how your system runs.

data.name: DescNvidia
data.value:
This module is reserved for recommended NVIDIA Control Panel settings: performance tweaks, power management and general or gaming optimization.
STATUS: COMING SOON This feature will be available in a future version of SYS_OPTIMIZER. Move to the next module to continue optimizing your system.

data.name: DescDeepTemp
data.value:
Deletes temporary system files stored in C:\Windows\Temp. Unlike %TEMP% which belongs to your user account, this folder is shared by Windows and system processes to store temporary work files.
RISK: LOW — REQUIRES ADMINISTRATOR These files are leftovers from system processes that have already finished. Windows will recreate them when needed. Files currently in use by active system services will be skipped automatically.

data.name: DescPrefetch
data.value:
Deletes .pf files stored in C:\Windows\Prefetch. Windows creates these files to help your programs load faster by remembering what resources each app needs when it starts.
RISK: LOW — REQUIRES ADMINISTRATOR Removing Prefetch files does not damage your system or your apps. The only noticeable effect is that programs may take slightly longer to open the first time after cleanup, while Windows rebuilds the Prefetch files automatically through normal use.

data.name: DescEvent
data.value:
Clears the Windows Event Log records: Application, Security, Setup and System. Windows keeps a history of system activity, app errors and security events in these logs. Over time they fill up with thousands of entries that take up space and make it harder to find useful information.
RISK: LOW — REQUIRES ADMINISTRATOR Clearing the logs does not affect how your system runs. Their main purpose is troubleshooting: if you suspect a problem or plan to contact tech support soon, consider skipping this module to keep your recent event history intact.

data.name: DescAdvDisk
data.value:
Advanced disk cleanup targeting three high-impact categories: leftover Windows Update downloads in SoftwareDistribution\Download, the Windows.old folder created when upgrading Windows versions, and accumulated system logs in C:\Windows\Logs.
RISK: MEDIUM — REQUIRES ADMINISTRATOR Windows.old can free up between 10 and 20 GB but its removal is permanent — you will no longer be able to roll back to your previous Windows version once deleted. Windows Update downloads will be re-downloaded if the system needs to reinstall an update. It is strongly recommended to create a restore point before running this module.
